history.content:
<thought>
O agente está virado para norte e a célula à frente (linha 6, coluna 4) é lava (L). Não é possível avançar. Virar no sentido horário faz com que o agente aponte para leste, onde a célula à direita (linha 7, coluna 5) é chão (.). Essa é uma ação segura para prosseguir.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly north of me is a floor ('.'). No goal is visible in the current view. Moving forward will allow me to explore new areas and possibly find the goal. The only safe adjacent cell is north, as west is lava and east is a wall.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>The cell directly in front of me (north) is empty and safe. The goal is not visible, so I will move forward to explore further.</thought>
<action>FRENTE</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom center, facing up. The cell directly in front is a floor ('.'), safe to move. The left and right cells are lava and wall respectively, so only forward is possible. Moving forward will allow further exploration.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at (7,4) facing up. The cell directly ahead (6,4) is floor, which is safe. The cells to the right (7,5) are wall and to the left (7,3) are lava, so no other safe immediate moves. I will move forward to explore new area and hopefully find the goal.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom row, facing north. The cell directly ahead is a floor, but beyond that, the path is blocked by a wall two steps ahead. To the east is a wall, and to the west are floor cells along the bottom row. Since there is a clear path to the west with multiple floor cells and unknown territory beyond, it is better to explore westward. I will rotate counter-clockwise to face west, then move west in the next step.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
I am at the bottom center facing up. The cell directly above me is floor, so I can move forward. I want to go up and then left to reach the goal, so I will start by moving up.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The goal is at the top-right of the map. My current direction is upward (north). The cell directly in front of me (row5, col3) is safe (floor). Moving forward will bring me closer to the goal and avoid lava. I will proceed forward.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north (upward). The cell directly ahead (row6, col4) is a floor (.) and safe. The goal O is at (row3, col6), which requires navigating through safe cells. Moving forward is a logical first step to approach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is a floor cell (`.`), so I can move forward safely. This will bring me one step closer to the goal. From there, I will need to navigate left to reach the column containing the safe path upward.
</thought>
<action>FRENTE</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward would be fatal. To the left is a floor cell, and from there I can navigate around the lava to reach the goal. Therefore, I need to turn counter-clockwise to face left.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The goal O is not visible in the current observation. The area above is blocked by a wall at row1, so moving upward leads to a dead end. The bottom row has floor cells to the left (col2, col1, col0) and to the right (col5) but the right path is blocked by lava at col4. To explore potentially new areas, I should first turn to face west (left) to move toward the left floor cells, which may reveal more of the map and possible paths downward.  
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The goal is not visible in the current observation. The cell directly ahead (north) is a floor cell, and there appears to be a clear vertical corridor of floor cells upward until a wall. Moving forward will allow me to explore further.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at row7 col4, facing north. The cell directly ahead is a safe floor cell. No goal is visible in the current view. To explore and potentially find the goal, I will move forward into the empty cell.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The goal is not visible in the current observation. The agent is facing north. Directly ahead is a safe empty cell at (6,4) which leads to a corridor on row5. Moving forward will allow us to explore new areas, potentially revealing the goal. There are no obstacles in front, so moving forward is a safe and logical step.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north. The cell directly in front is empty floor ('.'). The cells to the left and right are lava. The goal is not visible. Moving forward seems safe and will allow exploration.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front is a wall ('#'), so moving forward is not possible. The cells to the left and right are empty floor ('.'). I will turn clockwise to face east, which will allow me to move into the empty cell to my right and explore new areas.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The goal is not visible in the current observation. The agent is facing north (up), but moving forward leads to a vertical corridor that ends at a wall at row2. The floor to the right (east) at the same row is clear and may lead to new areas or the goal. Therefore, it is better to turn clockwise to face east and then move forward to explore the right side.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The goal is at the top row. The cell directly ahead (north) of my current position is a floor cell, so moving forward is safe and will progress towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is empty floor ('.'), which is safe. Moving forward will bring me closer to the goal, as the path leads north initially. I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
Looking at the current observation, the agent is at the bottom center (row7, col4) facing north. The cell directly ahead is floor (row6, col4), so it's safe to move forward. The goal is at row3, col6. A clear path exists by moving north twice, then west twice, then north twice, then east four times to the goal, avoiding lava. The first step is to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is a safe floor cell. I need to move forward to navigate around the lava cells and reach the goal. Moving forward is the first step in a longer path to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at (row7, col4) facing north. The cell directly ahead is lava, so I cannot move forward. The goal is at (row5, col6). To reach it, I need to go left (west) to navigate around the lava. The left side has a path: from (7,4) go west to (7,3), then (7,2), then north to (6,2), then north to (5,2), then east along row5 to the goal. Therefore, I should turn counter-clockwise to face west.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front (north) is a floor cell. Moving forward will allow me to gain a new observation and potentially see more of the map, including any goal or paths. It is a safe move.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
A célula à frente (acima) é um piso seguro. Mover para lá permite explorar mais o ambiente e possivelmente encontrar um caminho para o objetivo. Não há lava à frente, e as outras direções imediatas são lava ou desconhecidas. Portanto, avançar é a ação mais segura e informativa no momento.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>
